scenario.title: The Expert Persona
scenario.context: You need AI help explaining a technical concept (API rate limiting) to different audiences. The explanation needs to be tailored to the specific audience's knowledge level and needs.
scenario.goal: Write a prompt that establishes the right expert persona and provides enough context about the audience to get an appropriately tailored explanation.
constraints: Define who the AI should 'be' (their expertise)
Specify what the audience already knows
Include what the audience needs to achieve
scenario.exampleInput: Topic: API rate limiting. Audience: Marketing team members who need to understand why their automated reports sometimes fail.
hints: What kind of expert would be best at explaining this? A senior engineer? A technical writer?
What technical knowledge does a marketing team typically have?
What's the marketing team's actual goal - do they need deep understanding or just enough to solve their problem?

rubric.name: Expert Persona
rubric.description: Is an appropriate expert role established?

scoringGuide.excellent: Clear, appropriate expert role with relevant expertise defined

rubric.name: Audience Knowledge Level
rubric.description: Is the audience's existing knowledge clearly defined?

scoringGuide.excellent: Specific knowledge level with examples of what they know/don't know

rubric.name: Audience Needs
rubric.description: Are the audience's practical needs specified?

scoringGuide.excellent: Clear goal and how understanding helps achieve it
scoringGuide.good: Goal mentioned but not connected to explanation
scoringGuide.poor: No indication of why audience needs this

rubric.name: Tailoring Guidance
rubric.description: Does the prompt guide appropriate tailoring?

scoringGuide.excellent: Clear guidance on what to include/exclude for this audience

evaluatorContext.idealResponse:
Act as a friendly tech educator who specializes in explaining developer concepts to non-technical business teams.

Audience context: Marketing team members who use tools like Zapier and HubSpot but have no coding background. They understand 'APIs' as 'connections between apps' but don't know technical details. Their immediate problem: automated reports are failing with 'rate limit exceeded' errors.

Explain API rate limiting in a way that: 1) Uses analogies they'd understand (no code or technical jargon), 2) Focuses on WHY this affects their reports, 3) Gives them 2-3 practical things they can do to prevent failures. Keep it under 200 words - they need the practical solution, not a deep technical education.
commonMistakes: Not defining an expert persona at all
Choosing a persona too technical for the audience
Not connecting explanation to practical needs
antiPatterns: Asking for technical explanation for non-technical audience
No connection to real-world use case
